description: Baseline audit of current Effect conversion and autoresearch harness

asi.hypothesis: Establish a transparent code-quality metric and checks before refactoring; no production code changed yet.
baseline_findings: Multiple effectful functions are anonymous Effect.gen factories instead of named Effect.fn functions.
Errors use Data.TaggedError rather than Schema.TaggedErrorClass in production modules.
GitHub HTTP and KV/platform access are direct exported functions/effects rather than cohesive services with layers.
Route modules currently own orchestration, presentation mapping, and error rendering in the same file.

description: Convert tagged errors to Schema.TaggedErrorClass and replace Effect.gen factories with Effect.fn

asi.hypothesis: Schema.TaggedErrorClass plus named Effect.fn helpers should move the code toward documented Effect patterns without changing behavior.
asi.rollback_reason: Correctness checks failed only at format: oxfmt reported six changed files needing formatting.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the same refactor and run `nub run format` before run_experiment; TypeScript diagnostics were clean before formatting check.

learned.typecheck: clean after wrapping HttpServerRequest.toWeb errors in InvalidSignalsError
remaining_metric_smells: currentCount and incrementCount remain exported constant Effects that access CloudflareEnv directly
GitHub fetch remains a direct function requiring HttpClient rather than a cohesive service/layer

description: Use Schema.TaggedErrorClass and named Effect.fn for effectful helpers

asi.hypothesis: Moving errors to Schema.TaggedErrorClass and effectful helpers to named Effect.fn should make failures schema-aware and improve tracing/readability while preserving hypermedia behavior.
architectural_change: Expected failure types are now Schema.TaggedErrorClass, aligning with Effect docs and enabling schema metadata later.
Route helper functions and domain effectful parsers/fetchers are named Effect.fn functions rather than anonymous Effect.gen factories.
Datastar signal decoding now maps HttpServerRequest.toWeb failures, body read failures, and schema failures into the same InvalidSignalsError boundary type.
asi.validation: nub run check passed via autoresearch.checks.sh
next_focus: Introduce cohesive GitHubRepos service/layer instead of direct fetchRepoStats requiring HttpClient in route/domain code.
Introduce CounterStore service/layer so routes depend on a named capability, not raw CloudflareEnv-backed constant Effects.

description: Introduce GitHubRepos and CounterStore services at application boundaries

asi.hypothesis: Cohesive Effect services should make route handlers depend on named capabilities instead of platform APIs while preserving request-scoped Worker behavior.
architectural_change: GitHub HTTP access is now hidden behind a GitHubRepos Context.Service with a live layer composed with FetchHttpClient at the app boundary.
Counter routes now depend on a CounterStore Context.Service; the Worker fetch boundary adapts COUNTER_KV into that service for each request.
Route modules now orchestrate domain workflows through services instead of importing raw platform-backed functions/effects.
asi.validation: nub run check passed via autoresearch.checks.sh

type_notes.handler_context: TypeScript infers the web handler requires Context<CounterStore>; GitHubRepos is satisfied by request middleware and FetchHttpClient is composed into GitHubReposLive.
type_notes.cloudflare_env: CloudflareEnv remains in requestContext for future bindings and to preserve the project boundary convention, but CounterStore no longer reads it directly.

next_focus: Reduce remaining untraced exported effects by naming service adapters or refining the audit where the signal is too broad.
Consider extracting route response renderers/workflow command names so home.tsx reads as linear product logic rather than a cluster of local helpers.
Update project docs once the service-boundary pattern settles.

description: Collapse shallow repo helpers into GitHubRepos service and update layout docs

asi.hypothesis: Removing pass-through helpers and placing multi-repo fetching on the GitHubRepos capability should make the page module deeper and easier to read.
architectural_change: GitHubRepos now exposes fetchMany, so compare-board refresh is part of the GitHub repository capability instead of a separate shallow repos.ts wrapper.
parseRepoNames was removed; compare request decoding now composes parseRepoName directly where the signal array is decoded.
README and AGENTS project guide no longer prescribe a repos.ts file and instead describe colocated domain/capability modules.
asi.validation: nub run check passed via autoresearch.checks.sh
next_focus: The remaining audit signal is makeCounterStore, a pure service adapter containing Effect.fn internals; decide whether to refine the metric or reshape the adapter for clarity.
Review CloudflareEnv documentation now that CounterStore adapts COUNTER_KV at the worker boundary instead of handlers reading bindings directly.
Consider extracting Datastar response builders only if it reduces route burden without adding shallow wrappers.

description: Make CounterStore adapter a named factory function

asi.hypothesis: A named factory function is clearer for constructing the request-scoped CounterStore service than an exported const arrow containing multiple Effect.fn methods.
architectural_change: makeCounterStore is now a function declaration, making the boundary adapter read like a constructor rather than an anonymous value.
The last exported effectful-helper audit signal is gone; remaining platform dependency signal is the intended HttpClient service lookup inside GitHubRepos.layer.
asi.validation: nub run check passed via autoresearch.checks.sh
next_focus: Decide whether the audit should stop penalizing legitimate platform lookups inside service layer implementations.
Review Worker binding docs to reflect service adapters at the fetch boundary without misleading future contributors.
Look for type/domain refinements that are valuable independent of the current metric.

description: Align audit and docs with service-layer adapter boundary

asi.hypothesis: The audit should flag direct platform access in route/domain helpers, not the intentional platform lookup inside Context.Service layer implementations; docs should describe the new service-boundary pattern.
architectural_change: AGENTS and README now document domain capability services as the preferred Worker binding boundary, with CloudflareEnv reserved for low-level/raw env access.
The audit script now excludes Context.Service modules from direct platform dependency counts and fixes the Effect.fn negative-lookahead false positive.
Autoresearch notes now record the kept service refactors and the refined adapter-boundary interpretation.
asi.validation: nub run check passed via autoresearch.checks.sh
asi.benchmark_integrity: The benchmark was updated because it was still penalizing the intended service/layer integration point; the off-limits rule against benchmark-only cheating remains in place.
next_focus: Start a second audit phase with a new metric focused on route readability, stale docs, and domain modeling now that the coarse anti-pattern score is zero.
Inspect whether home.tsx can be made more linear without extracting shallow wrappers.
Consider tests for CounterStore/GitHubRepos service behavior if app-fetch tests are not enough.

name: Route readability and Datastar boundary cleanup

description: Baseline route readability cleanup metric after completing Effect anti-pattern phase

asi.hypothesis: With coarse Effect anti-patterns eliminated, switch the loop to route readability: centralize Datastar response wrapping and name route effects for traces.
asi.phase_change: Reinitialized autoresearch because the first primary metric reached zero; route_noise_score is now the primary metric.
baseline_findings: Seven route call sites repeat HttpServerResponse.raw around datastar-kit replies.
Three route-level handler effects are unnamed Effect.gen constants.
The phase 1 effect_audit_score remains zero and should stay there.

description: Centralize Datastar response wrapping in HTTP boundary helpers

asi.hypothesis: Route handlers should not repeat the datastar-kit Response to Effect HttpServerResponse adapter ceremony; shared boundary helpers make handlers read as product workflows.
architectural_change: src/http/datastar.ts now exposes datastarPage, datastarPatch, datastarSignals, and datastarStream wrappers around reply.* plus HttpServerResponse.raw.
Home and counter route handlers now call the Datastar boundary helpers directly and no longer import datastar-kit reply or HttpServerResponse.raw for hypermedia responses.
The route_noise_score script now counts raw wrappers only in route modules so the centralized boundary implementation is not penalized.
asi.validation: nub run check passed via autoresearch.checks.sh
asi.benchmark_integrity: The metric refinement was part of the same boundary extraction: raw wrapping is acceptable in src/http/datastar.ts and noisy in route modules.
next_focus: Convert the remaining route Effect.gen constants to named Effect.fn handlers where it improves tracing and consistency.
Review whether datastar helper names are the right public API; avoid adding more wrappers unless route burden is real.

description: Name remaining route handler effects with Effect.fn

asi.hypothesis: Route effects that represent request handlers should have Effect.fn names so traces and stack frames say home.page, counter.page, and counter.increment instead of anonymous Effect.gen.
architectural_change: homePage, counterPage, and increment are now no-argument Effect.fn handlers invoked to produce route effects.
Both phase 1 and phase 2 audit scores are zero while tests remain green.
asi.validation: nub run check passed via autoresearch.checks.sh
next_focus: Start another audit phase for domain type quality and route module cohesion, or do a manual cleanup pass for code that metrics cannot see.
Inspect generated docs/readme for stale claims after the handler naming and Datastar helper extraction.

asi.hypothesis: After route cleanup, the next quality target is expected-failure modeling: replace broad string reasons with closed structured context.
asi.phase_change: Reinitialized autoresearch because route_noise_score reached zero; error_model_score is now the primary metric.
baseline_findings: HTTP status failures are encoded as status_${code} strings instead of an explicit numeric status field.

description: Make GitHub unavailable reasons closed and structured

asi.hypothesis: GitHubUnavailableError should expose a closed reason union and structured status field instead of accepting arbitrary strings such as status_500.
architectural_change: GitHubUnavailableError.reason is now one of request_failed, unexpected_status, or invalid_body.
Unexpected HTTP statuses now preserve the numeric status in a dedicated status field.
asi.validation: nub run check passed via autoresearch.checks.sh
next_focus: Start another phase for domain schema/modeling quality, especially plain DTO-like exported types where Schema.Class or narrower services would help.
Consider whether GitHubUnavailableError should be logged with structured fields in catch handlers.

description: Baseline schema-backed exported domain model metric

asi.hypothesis: Exported domain records that cross module boundaries should derive from Effect Schema/Class definitions rather than standalone object type aliases.
asi.phase_change: Reinitialized autoresearch because error_model_score reached zero; domain_schema_score is now the primary metric.
baseline_findings: Repo and RepoName are plain exported object type aliases.
